name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-9
title: scope-guard false positive: parses bash command text for write paths, triggers on description argument content
description:
scope-guard scans the full bash command string for write-path patterns, causing false positives when non-path text resembles an absolute path.

Observed: filing a bug via file-item.py with a long --description argument caused scope-guard to deny the command, extracting the path /home/cyxu/workflow-dev/rabbit-marathon/ws26/). from the trailing characters of the description text. The script was not writing to that path.

Root cause: scope-guard's path detection regex is too broad — it matches path-like substrings inside quoted string arguments rather than limiting to actual write destinations.

Fix: narrow path extraction to actual write operations only (e.g. Write tool calls, cat/echo redirections, mv/cp destinations) and exclude text inside quoted --flag arguments. Alternatively, validate that extracted paths exist or are being opened for write before triggering the deny.

history.note: Fixed as part of RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-8 work cycle (PR #102). Quote stripping moved from per-segment to full-command-before-split in extract_bash_targets(), eliminating false-positive path detection on --description argument text containing ;|& characters.
